steps.title: Make the exponent zero
steps.content: The coefficient displays $f(1)$ when the exponential factor equals $1$ at $x=1$. That happens when the exponent becomes $0$ at $x=1$.

steps.title: Check the displayed coefficient
steps.content: For the form $f(x)=144(2)^{x-1}$, substituting $x=1$ gives $$f(1)=144(2)^{1-1}=144(2)^0=144$$ The coefficient is the value of $k$. The answer is C.
